articles.title: Shree Ram Twistex IPO Day 3: Oversubscribed 12x; GMP jumps to 17%. Check key details
articles.summary: Shree Ram Twistex's IPO has seen overwhelming investor interest, being oversubscribed 12 times. Non-Institutional Investors and retail participants showed significant demand. The company plans to use the funds for renewable energy projects and debt repayment. Share allotment is expected soon, with a tentative listing on March 2.

articles.title: IT under pressure, energy & bank PSUs may deliver 25–30% upside: Gautam Shah
articles.summary: IT stocks face structural headwinds, with valuations and AI disruption posing risks, according to Gautam Shah. He advises against catching falling knives in IT, predicting Nifty IT's potential fall to 26,000. Instead, he favors PSU banks, metals, and energy sectors, anticipating significant upside due to value rotation and geopolitical factors.

articles.title: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei rises to a record, boosted by reflationist nominations for BOJ board
articles.summary: Japan's ​Nikkei share average climbed to another record on Wednesday, ​driven by a surge in tech-related stocks and waning expectations of an imminent policy rate hike after new central bank board member nominations.

articles.title: Waaree Energies says US manufacturing to insulate 126% solar import duty blow. Check more details
articles.summary: Waaree Energies Share: The United States has imposed significant preliminary duties on solar imports from India, Indonesia, and Laos. This action follows a determination that these countries unfairly subsidize their solar manufacturing sectors. Waaree Energies, an Indian company, expects its expanding US manufacturing to mitigate the impact of these tariffs.

articles.title: Global Markets | Australian shares hit record high as investors focus on strong earnings, shrug off hot CPI
articles.summary: Australian shares reached a record high, driven by strong blue-chip earnings from companies like Woolworths and WiseTech. Despite a hotter-than-expected inflation report, the market focused on robust corporate performance, with miners also contributing to the gains. Investors are now anticipating a potential rate hike from the central bank.

articles.title: Angel One 1:10 stock split tomorrow: What shareholders should know
articles.summary: Angel One has set February 26 as the record date for its 1:10 stock split, a move aimed at enhancing liquidity and accessibility for retail investors. Shareholders holding the stock on this date will receive 10 shares for every one held, with the face value of each share reduced.

articles.title: Decoded: The viral doomsday AI memo that roiled Wall Street
articles.summary: A viral 7,000-word Substack essay by Citrini Research founder James van Geelen sparked a sharp selloff on Wall Street by outlining a hypothetical 2028 “Global Intelligence Crisis.” The scenario imagines AI wiping out white-collar jobs, crushing software revenues, straining credit and housing markets, and triggering a deflationary economic cascade.

articles.title: Anthropic's 'misanthropic' moment for Indian IT! Claude parent's valuation tops the whole industry
articles.summary: Amid the rapid rise of artificial intelligence, AI firm Anthropic — valued at about $380 billion — has overtaken the combined market capitalisation of India’s top IT companies, including TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL Technologies and Tech Mahindra, which together are worth around $240 billion.

articles.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum jump 3% in one day as Trump’s State of the Union address lifts crypto sentiment
articles.summary: Bitcoin and Ethereum saw a 3% surge on Wednesday, driven by positive sentiment following President Trump's State of the Union address. This rebound followed a choppy week, with major altcoins also experiencing gains. The crypto market capitalization rose to $2.25 trillion, reflecting improved macro sentiment and policy continuity.

articles.title: Piyush Pandey sees buying opportunity in IT stocks despite AI fears
articles.summary: Piyush Pandey believes AI disruption fears for Indian IT stocks are overblown, with current valuations being "extremely comfortable." He sees long-term opportunities outweighing short-term volatility, predicting improved performance in FY27 compared to FY26 due to increased productivity and a shift to outcome-based projects.

articles.title: Softer dollar, modest inflows offer breathing room to Indian rupee
articles.summary: The Indian rupee held steady near 90.92 against the dollar, supported by a weaker greenback and foreign bank sales. Local stocks, particularly IT shares, rebounded, contributing to modest dollar inflows. Global markets were buoyed by AI enthusiasm and anticipation surrounding U.S. President Trump's address, though trade uncertainty persists.

articles.title: Indian bonds rise after strong buying sparks RBI participation bets
articles.summary: Indian government bonds saw a rise today. Strong buying from the 'others' investor category boosted prices. This group includes insurers, pension funds, and potentially the Reserve Bank of India. Such purchases suggest increased liquidity in the market. This activity could lead to further price gains for bonds. The central bank has been actively infusing liquidity this financial year.

articles.title: Rs 3 lakh crore added! 5 key factors behind 700-point Sensex surge, Nifty above 25,600
articles.summary: Indian stock markets saw a significant rebound on Wednesday. The Sensex and Nifty 50 indices surged, recovering from a previous day's sharp decline. This rally boosted the market capitalization of BSE-listed firms substantially. Key sectors like IT and Metal led the gains. Several major companies contributed to the upward movement, indicating a positive market sentiment.

articles.title: Chakri Lokapriya sees value in cyclical and PSU stocks despite market volatility
articles.summary: Market strategist Chakri Lokapriya identifies selective opportunities in cyclical sectors, railways, defence, and PSU banks, citing strong order books and reasonable valuations for long-term investments. While near-term capex may be subdued due to US Supreme Court ruling uncertainty, these sectors offer compelling growth prospects.

articles.title: Massive AI capex not a bubble, but software & IT business models at risk: Arvind Sanger
articles.summary: Massive AI investments by US tech giants are unlikely to be a bubble, but could fundamentally disrupt traditional software and IT services, including Indian firms. Arvind Sanger of Geosphere Capital believes AI adoption presents a structural wave of value creation, not speculation. The real risk lies in business model disruption, potentially impacting pricing and revenue for manpower-heavy IT services.

articles.title: Mobilise App SME IPO Day 3: Check GMP, subscription status and key details
articles.summary: Mobilise App Lab’s Rs 20.10 crore SME IPO has drawn strong investor interest, with 12x subscription by Day 2 and a grey market premium of Rs 8 signaling a potential Rs 88 listing. The SaaS company aims to fund product development, expansion, and talent acquisition as it prepares to debut on NSE Emerge.

articles.title: Global Market | Hot Streak or Turning Point? Kospi’s historic run leaves investors weighing what comes next
articles.summary: South Korea's KOSPI index is surging towards historic highs, driven by tech leadership in AI and semiconductors, alongside policy reforms aimed at improving corporate governance and shareholder returns. This rally, exceeding 40% this year, is sparking debate on its sustainability, with some investors expressing caution amid short-selling activity and global macro concerns.

articles.title: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warns AI and 'dumb things' can trigger a 2008-like crisis
articles.summary: Jamie Dimon warned markets resemble pre-2008 conditions, citing risky behaviour, elevated asset prices and AI-driven disruption. He said people are doing “dumb things” to make money, warned of cyclical downturns ahead, and cautioned that enthusiasm around artificial intelligence could destabilise software and technology sectors globally over time.

articles.title: Vedanta shares rise 5% as BofA upgrades stock to Buy, raises target price by 75%. Here’s why
articles.summary: Vedanta Ltd shares surged upto 5% on Wednesday after BofA Securities upgraded the stock to “Buy” from “Neutral” and raised its target price sharply to Rs 840 from Rs 480. The brokerage turned positive on the back of a stronger outlook for aluminium and supportive silver prices, along with an estimated FY27 dividend yield of over 6%.

articles.title: Global Market | Jonathan Schiessl on how investors can navigate global market volatility
articles.summary: Global markets are witnessing a significant rotation from mega-cap tech to real assets, cyclicals, and industrials. In India, IT faces AI disruption fears, while banking and defensive sectors gain traction. Opportunities are emerging in pharmaceuticals' GLP-1 generics and attractive metals valuations.

articles.title: Airtel’s ₹14,000 crore NBFC bet small vs ₹60,000 crore FCF; dividend upside likely: IIFL Capital
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel is entering lending with a new NBFC. This move is a modest allocation of its strong free cash flows. The company plans significant investments in data centres and home broadband. Investors are watching execution risks in this new venture. Airtel's balance sheet is strengthening, allowing for growth and shareholder returns. Higher dividends are expected.

articles.title: IBM stock in spotlight after sharp moves. Is volatility creating opportunity?
articles.summary: IBM shares saw a sharp 13% drop as fears of AI disrupting legacy systems resurfaced, wiping out significant market value. While some see this as an overreaction, the market debates AI's impact on IBM's core businesses. Analysts suggest resilient profit pools and IBM's AI integration strategy could offer a tactical entry point for investors.

articles.title: Stocks to buy: Adani Ports, HAL, Titan among Prabhudas Lilladher's 14 top picks
articles.summary: Prabhudas Lilladher has revealed its top stock picks. The brokerage highlights fourteen companies, including Adani Ports, HAL, and Titan. Analysts have set new price targets for these stocks. The firm remains optimistic about sectors like banking and capital goods. Investors can explore these recommendations for potential growth opportunities.

articles.title: Sensex rallies over 700 points, Nifty above 25,600 as IT stocks bounce back
articles.summary: Indian stock markets opened higher on Wednesday. Tech stocks bounced back after yesterday's sharp decline. Sensex and Nifty 50 saw gains. IT and Metal sectors led the recovery. Foreign investors were net sellers, while domestic investors bought equities. US markets also closed higher. Crude oil prices increased amid supply concerns.

articles.title: US Stock Market | US consumer confidence rebounds, but labour concerns linger : What it means for Wall Street
articles.summary: US consumer confidence unexpectedly rose in February, driven by younger demographics, yet concerns about job availability persist, reaching a five-year high. This mixed sentiment, influenced by policy uncertainty, presents a complex outlook for the Wall Street as investors weigh improved confidence against potential labor market softening.

articles.title: Once a 150% multibagger, One Mobikwik shares now trade 26% below IPO price. Time to exit?
articles.summary: Fintech firm One MobiKwik Systems saw its stock surge on BSE's approval for its subsidiary's broking operations, though gains were mostly trimmed. The company, which offers digital wallets and payment services, has faced prolonged underperformance since its IPO. MobiKwik returned to profitability in the last quarter, reporting its highest revenue in six quarters.

articles.title: Silver prices jump Rs 7,200, gold reclaims Rs 1.6 lakh as tariff, geopolitical uncertainty looms. What are experts saying?
articles.summary: Gold reclaimed the Rs 1.6 lakh mark while silver surged Rs 7,200 on the MCX amid tariff uncertainty and geopolitical tensions. Experts expect continued volatility, with support from global cues and dollar movement, while advising cautious trading strategies as markets react to evolving macroeconomic and geopolitical developments.

articles.title: Asian Paints Share Price Live Updates: Asian Paints experiences decline under moving average
Dr Reddys Share Price Live Updates: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories experiences a slight drop in share price
Dr. Reddy's Labs shares in focus after US SEC closes probe into bribery claims against firm
articles.summary: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories shares are in focus today. The US SEC has concluded its investigation into allegations of improper payments to healthcare professionals. The regulator has decided not to recommend any enforcement action against the company. This development follows an internal probe launched by Dr. Reddy's in 2020. The company fully cooperated with the authorities during the investigation.

articles.title: A six-month slump for a former multibagger: What is going on with Eternal?
articles.summary: Eternal, once a star stock, faces a slump. Competition in food delivery and quick commerce intensifies. Valuations were high, and a leadership change added uncertainty. While core businesses improve, investors seek predictable earnings. The company's strong cash position offers long-term support. Analysts suggest cautious accumulation.

articles.title: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery IPO Day 2: GMP, subscription status, brokerages’ views & key details
articles.summary: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery’s Rs 380 crore IPO enters Day 2 with 59% subscription and a grey market premium of Rs 4–5, implying modest listing gains. While institutional participation remains strong, retail demand is moderate. Brokerages remain divided, balancing healthy margins against valuation and execution risks.

articles.title: IRFC shares decline 4% as government plans to divest 4% via OFS. Check floor price, other details
articles.summary: The government will sell up to 4% of its stake in Indian Railway Finance Corporation. The Offer for Sale begins Wednesday for institutional investors and Thursday for retail investors. The floor price is set at Rs 104 per share. IRFC is the funding arm for Indian Railways. The company recently reported its highest-ever quarterly profit.

articles.title: Time for lumpsum investment in multi-cap and flexi-cap funds: Union AMC's Harshad Patwardhan
articles.summary: With improving earnings visibility and more reasonable valuations, investors can deploy fresh capital in diversified equity strategies. Multi-cap and flexi-cap funds are well-positioned to capture opportunities across market segments. Broad-based growth is expected, driven by government initiatives and potential trade deal benefits.

articles.title: Aequs to invest ₹230 crore in consumer electronics arm; aerospace order book at $814 million
articles.summary: Aequs is investing ₹230 crore in its consumer electronics division to cut debt and boost capital expenditure. The company's aerospace segment shows strong revenue visibility with an $814 million order book. A new facility in Tamil Nadu will focus on aero engine components. Aequs aims for sustained growth by strengthening aerospace and scaling consumer electronics.

articles.title: ETMarkets Smart Talk | From stop-loss discipline to mental strength: Vishal Malkan’s 30-year market playbook
articles.summary: After three decades in markets, Vishal Malkan emphasizes survival over prediction, highlighting psychology as key to long-term trading success. He stresses discipline, adaptability, patience, and focus, advocating for risk management and capital preservation as paramount. Malkan believes mental resilience, akin to a cricketer protecting their wicket, is crucial for navigating market volatility and achieving sustained profitability.

articles.title: Asia and select Europe markets look attractive amid US concentration risks: David Gibson-Moore
articles.summary: US equities have seen strong gains driven by AI mega-caps. Concerns about market concentration and high valuations are prompting investors to look beyond the US. Asia, including India, South Korea, and Indonesia, shows improving fundamentals. Select European markets, particularly manufacturing and defence, also present compelling opportunities for diversification in the coming months.

articles.title: IT stock crash wipes out Rs 1.2 lakh crore for LIC & mutual funds in bloodbath not seen since 2008
articles.summary: India's top financial institutions, LIC and mutual funds, have suffered significant losses as the Nifty IT index plunged 21%. Fears of AI disrupting the traditional IT labour-arbitrage model are driving this downturn, with analysts questioning the long-term growth prospects of major IT firms. Investors face a dilemma between beaten-down valuations and uncertain future fundamentals.

articles.title: Afcons Infrastructure shares tumble 4% after client terminates contract worth €113 million
articles.summary: Afcons Infrastructure's shares slipped 4% following a contract termination notice from Société Autoroutière du Gabon. The €113.03 million project for National Road NR1 in Gabon is nearing completion. Afcons disputes the termination, asserting it is inconsistent with contract terms. The company is pursuing legal remedies.

articles.title: Infosys Share Price Live Updates: Infosys ends the day at Rs 1327.5, down 3.92%
Clean Max Enviro Energy IPO Day 3: Check GMP, subscription status, brokerage outlook and key highlights
articles.summary: The Rs 3,100 crore Clean Max Enviro Energy IPO enters its final bidding day with 45% subscription so far and a grey market premium of just Rs 1–3, indicating limited listing gains. While institutional demand is firm, retail participation remains muted. Brokerages remain divided on valuation and long-term prospects.

articles.title: Infosys, TCS lead IT rebound; stocks jump up to 5% after five-day rout
articles.summary: Indian IT stocks rose as much as 5%, sapping a 5-day losing streak after Anthropic announced enterprise partnerships, easing fears of AI-led disruption. Heavy recent selling pushed Nifty IT into oversold territory, prompting contrarian interest. While technical indicators hint at potential stabilisation, analysts warn that downside risks persist, with defined support and resistance levels guiding near-term expectations for investors.

articles.title: Waaree, Premier Energies plunge up to 14% after US slaps 126% initial solar import duty
articles.summary: Indian solar manufacturers like Waaree Energies and Vikram Solar faced significant stock drops after the US imposed hefty preliminary duties of up to 126% on imports from India. Citing unfair subsidies, these tariffs could severely limit access to the crucial US market for Indian companies, impacting their export-oriented businesses and investment plans.

articles.title: Waaree Energies among 4 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
articles.summary: Four Nifty500 stocks, including Waaree Energies and AU Small Finance Bank, showed a bullish RSI upswing on February 24 after gaining over 2%. The RSI crossing above 50 signals strengthening price momentum, indicating potential upward movement and helping traders identify emerging buying opportunities based on technical scan data from StockEdge.

articles.title: Natco Pharma among 7 stocks flash bullish signals, indicating possible uptrend
articles.summary: Seven Nifty500 stocks, including Natco Pharma and Bandhan Bank, formed a White Marubozu pattern on February 24, signalling strong buying interest. The bullish candlestick formation, identified through StockEdge technical scans, indicates that buyers dominated trading through the session, suggesting potential near-term upward momentum in these counters.

articles.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: IOC, Bharti Airtel among 5 stocks that could give 10-40% return
articles.summary: We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources.

articles.title: Mukul Agrawal, Sunil Singhania-backed Yaap Digital IPO opens today. Check GMP and other details
articles.summary: Yaap Digital's Rs 80.11 crore IPO opens today, with a price band of Rs 138-145 per share. Backed by prominent investors Mukul Agrawal and Sunil Singhania, the company aims to fund acquisitions and expand its AI-driven marketing services. The issue closes on February 27, with listing on NSE SME platform scheduled for March 5.

articles.title: Omnitech Engineering IPO opens for subscription. Check brokerages review, GMP and other details
articles.summary: Omnitech Engineering's IPO is now open for subscription. The precision engineering firm plans to raise Rs 583 crore. The issue closes on February 27. Shares are priced between Rs 216 and Rs 227. The company aims for a listing on BSE and NSE. Grey market premium suggests a mild listing pop. Financial performance shows strong growth.

articles.title: Yashhtej Industries shares list at 20% discount to IPO price on BSE SME platform
articles.summary: Yashhtej Industries made a weak BSE SME debut, listing at Rs 88—20% below its IPO price of Rs 110 and underperforming GMP expectations. Despite 1.37x overall subscription and strong retail interest, the stock slipped. The soybean crude oil processor has posted sharp financial growth and plans major capex ahead.

articles.title: Negative Breakout: These 12 stocks cross below their 200 DMAs
articles.summary: Twelve stocks' close prices crossed below their 200 DMA (Daily Moving Averages) on February 24, according to stockedge.com's technical scan data.
